name: Operations
description: Cross-machine fleet view for the qontinui-web app. Surfaces the page header, the cross-repo dependency-graph entry point, and the coord-driven device-status tile. Authored against the SPEC_CI (ci-bot) account in Visual mode: an empty fleet with no devices reporting, so the device-status tile renders its 'No devices reporting status' empty state (not machine rows) and the WS-offline polling indicator. Asserts structural anchors present whether or not the fleet has data.

metadata.expectedServerErrorsNote: GENUINELY-UNAVOIDABLE-IN-CI (kept after the 2026-05-25 baseline-500 triage; NOT a fixable backend defect like the ai-tasks/runs/automation-builder waivers, which were removed). GET /coord-api/status is the Next same-origin proxy (next.config.mjs: /coord-api/:path* -> ${COORD_URL}/coord/:path*). In CI COORD_URL is unset, so it defaults to http://localhost:9870 with no coord running -> the proxy 500s. The coord SERVICE is healthy (coord.staging.qontinui.io/coord/status returns 200); the 500 is purely CI infra absence. Wiring COORD_URL to the live staging coord is NOT a valid fix: this spec's 'operations-device-status-empty' state asserts the EMPTY-fleet tile, but the shared staging coord carries transient real devices (e.g. a manual-test-coord session), so a live coord makes the assertion non-deterministic (populated tile -> no_candidates). A deterministic fix needs a CI-local coord stub returning an empty fleet (follow-up). Until then this single same-origin 5xx is expected; any OTHER same-origin 5xx on this spec still reds the gate.

states.name: Page header
states.description: Top-of-page banner - always rendered regardless of backend availability or fleet state.

assertions.description: h1 Operations heading anchors the page.
Cross-machine subtitle paragraph anchors the page's purpose.

states.name: Cross-repo dependency-graph entry point
states.description: Operator-driven cross-repo dependency-graph mini-form (PR Merge Orchestrator Phase 5 D5.5). Always rendered - no auth gating, no data dependency.

assertions.description: Section h4 names the cross-repo dependencies panel.
Inline link routes to the full dependency-graph view.
Repo (owner/name) text input is present so the operator can select a connected component.
PR number input completes the (repo, pr) tuple.
Submit button loads the connected component into the embedded graph.

states.name: Device status tile
states.description: Voluntary 'what is each machine doing right now' broadcast surfaced from GET /coord/status (proxied via /coord-api/status) and live-updated via the coord WebSocket. Asserts the tile's structural anchors - the section root, the heading, the WS-vs-polling connection indicator, and the refresh-status button - not specific machine rows (which vary per fleet state).

assertions.description: Refresh-status button is reachable by operators when WS is down or stale.
Connection-state indicator (live vs polling-fallback) is mounted next to the tile header.

states.name: Device status empty state
states.description: With no devices reporting (the empty SPEC_CI fleet), the tile renders an empty-state notice instead of a row list. Asserts the empty-state container and the POST /coord/status code hint operators use to populate it.

assertions.description: Empty-state container explains that no devices are reporting status.
Code hint names the POST /coord/status endpoint agents use to broadcast.
